<CLIENT> has observed significant operational friction within her organization?s warranty and service workflows, which are triggered when homeowners contact the company directly to report service issues or file warranty claims. Although products are sold and installed through a dealer network, the manufacturer becomes the primary point of contact for customers, creating a quasi B2C service model layered on top of a complex dealer ecosystem. Service teams must manually trace product history, confirm installation details (often dating back five or more years), identify the original dealer, and account for changes such as dealer acquisitions or homeowner turnover. This investigative work is highly time-intensive and creates delays that impact both productivity and customer satisfaction. <CLIENT> is interested in learning from organizations that have successfully modernized similar warranty operations, particularly through AI-assisted workflows that improve efficiency while maintaining strong customer outcomes. She is looking for peer insights and practical examples to understand how AI and automation can support claim intake, historical tracing, dealer identification, and decision support for service agents. The goal is to help service teams resolve claims faster, reduce manual effort, and balance customer experience with operational and business constraints.
